hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2 selectivity by forming specific interactions with SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 regions while avoiding off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by introducing mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions. The focus will be on positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 with suggested mutations to reduce off-target binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: To enhance SSTR2 selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in off-targets (SSTR3/SSTR5) at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing on these positions allows us to introduce mutations that reduce off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues.

These mutations aim to disrupt conserved pocket interactions in off-targets without affecting the pharmacophore (positions 7-10) or critical residues (Cys3/Cys14). Introducing polar or charged residues at these positions can reduce off-target affinity.
